column29: Yes, Juniper rejected me because I am a type 2 diabetic and currently on Ozempic.  However, that should not exclude me from joining Juniper.  Losing weight is multi-faceted, as Juniper well knows, and just because I take Ozempic for my diabetes does not mean that I have lost weight, or that I am losing weight.  It was very short-sighted in my opinion, of Juniper, to just reject me before even considering my unique circumstances.  To be completely honest, I think Juniper judged me and discriminated against me because I was already on Ozempic for diabetes - not for weight loss.  They are 2 completely different things.

column29: Where to start: "We built Juniper to help women lose weight and keep it off..." But Juniper wouldn't help me.  Instead, I had a dr/consultant (Male-via an email) give me an answer straight away that said "have shakes", completely missing any questions that a GP might consider or ask. I'm in pre-menopause, having to take HRT, my weight has ballooned by 10kg in less than 10 months, I'm moderately active (albeit limited due to a back operation), I consider myself a good eater, but here I am feeling more depressed than ever, too scared to put on my work clothes or to be seen in public.  I'm not obese by any means, but still overweight, despite everything I do. I'm quite upset that i got this survey, when, as i said, you wouldn't help me, yet I had no opportunity for a voice at the time, just online, assumption-led service.
